headline: FED RATE CUT PUSHED TO LATE 2026 AS WAR-DRIVEN INFLATION LINGERS — GOLD LIKES THE DELAY
snippet: A Reuters economist poll now places the Fed's first cut in late 2026, pushed back by war-related inflation risks including elevated energy prices tied to the Strait of Hormuz blockage. Delayed easing historically supports non-yielding hard assets. Gold bulls argue the macro setup — sticky inflation plus record central bank buying — remains intact regardless of Fed timing.

headline: AVINO SILVER & GOLD SHIPS 568,112 OZ IN Q1 2026, TARGETS STRONGER Q2 THROUGHPUT
snippet: Avino Silver & Gold (TSX-V: ASM) reported Q1 2026 silver-equivalent production of 568,112 ounces and is guiding for higher throughput in Q2. With industry-average silver AISC near $12/oz against a $76+ spot price, silver producers are running near-record margins. Full quarterly financials are expected in the coming weeks.

headline: SUN SUMMIT'S NI 43-101 MAPS EXPANDING MULTI-ZONE GOLD-SILVER SYSTEM IN BRITISH COLUMBIA
snippet: Sun Summit Minerals (TSX-V: SMN) filed an NI 43-101 technical report for its JD Project in BC documenting an expanding multi-zone gold-silver system and new drill-defined targets. The report is a regulatory milestone ahead of the company's 2026 summer drill program, with a resource update targeted for H2 2026.

headline: GRADE BOMB: GR SILVER HITS 15.6M AT 351 G/T SILVER — EXTENDS ZONE 500M DOWN-DIP IN MEXICO
snippet: GR Silver Mining (TSX-V: GRSL) intercepted 15.6 metres at 351 g/t silver — including 2.5m at 1,395 g/t Ag — at the San Marcial area of its Plomosas Project in Sinaloa, Mexico. The intercept extends high-grade mineralisation 500 metres down dip from surface and confirms structural continuity on step-out. Two rigs are turning with a third en route for the 20,000m campaign.

headline: KAMLOOPS JUNIOR GOLD: 2026 EXPLORATION PROGRAM UPDATE AND NEW PRICE TARGETS SET
snippet: Streetwise Reports covers the 2026 exploration update and revised technical price targets for Kamloops Junior Gold, a TSX-V-listed BC explorer. The analyst notes improved chart structure and an upgraded resource outlook heading into summer drill season, with gold above $4,700 providing strong incentive to drill.

headline: COPPER RETREATS TO $6.02/LB — GOLDMAN HOLDS $12,650/T, TRAXYS MAKES THE BULL CASE FOR $15,000
snippet: Copper fell 0.86% to $6.02/lb on April 24 as Chinese surplus data and pre-holiday restocking capped a strong April. Goldman Sachs held its $12,650/tonne year-end forecast despite projecting a 490,000-tonne refined surplus. Traxys takes a more aggressive stance, arguing sustained Chinese smelter production cuts through Q4 could push the metal back toward $15,000.

headline: LITHIUM CARBONATE UP 40% IN CHINA YTD — CNY 160,000/T AS CATL'S JIANXIAWO MINE STAYS DARK
snippet: Battery-grade lithium carbonate in China has rallied approximately 40% year-to-date toward CNY 160,000/tonne, driven by the ongoing closure of CATL's 65,000-tonne Jianxiawo mine and the April 1 cut to VAT export rebates on lithium-ion batteries. ASX lithium stocks surged as traders priced in the first potential supply deficit in two years. Zimbabwe's concentrate export ban adds further supply risk.

headline: GOLD, SILVER, COPPER: "STRUCTURAL BULL INTACT" — ANALYSTS STAY LONG INTO SUMMER DRILL SEASON
snippet: An April 23 market commentary argues the structural bull case for gold, silver and copper is unbroken despite near-term volatility. Gold is anchored by central bank buying near 60 tonnes/month; silver by a sixth consecutive supply deficit year; copper by AI infrastructure buildout and long-dated mine supply gaps. The piece recommends maintaining exposure to junior explorers ahead of summer drilling.
